74.reason: [The loop condition should be i < v, not i <= v, because v is the count of terms. Using i <= v will print one extra term with uninitialized values (0 0), causing the buggy output "0 0" at the end.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The loop condition should be i < v, not i <= v, because v is the count of terms. Using i <= v will print one extra term with uninitialized values (0 0), causing the buggy output "0 0" at the end.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition should be i < v, not i <= v, because v is the count of valid terms. Using <= causes an extra iteration printing uninitialized or zero values, leading to "0 0" at the end.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop should be i<v to avoid printing uninitialized coe[v] and ind[v]. This causes the extra "0 0" output.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Print loop condition i<=v prints an extra term (coe[v] and ind[v] are uninitialized zeros). Should be i<v.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop condition should be i < v. v is the count of terms. Accessing index v is out of bounds and prints uninitialized/zero values, causing the "0 0" in the output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: Loop condition should be i < v. Using <= accesses index v which is out of bounds for the valid data stored in coe/ind (indices 0 to v-1), printing garbage or zero values.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [should be i < v; i <= v prints an extra uninitialized term (index v), which explains the "0 0" in output]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [prints v+1 terms (0 to v inclusive), but only v terms were stored (indices 0 to v-1); this prints an extra uninitialized term (coe[v], ind[v]) which is zero-initialized, causing "0 0" in output]

36.reason: Loop condition should be u<v to avoid accessing uninitialized ind[v] (when v is the count of existing terms). This can cause incorrect matching or out-of-bounds access.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop condition should be u<v to avoid accessing uninitialized ind[v] (when v is the count of existing terms). This can cause incorrect matching or out-of-bounds access.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [u <= v accesses uninitialized element coe[v], ind[v]; should be u < v]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [u <= v accesses ind[v], which is uninitialized (since valid indices are 0 to v-1); should be u < v]

60.reason: Sorting loop accesses ind[e+1] out of bounds when e = v-i-1 (the last iteration). It should be e < v-i-1.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  When v-i is 1, e=0 accesses ind[e+1]=ind[1] which is out of bounds for the last element. This causes undefined behavior and introduces extra zero terms.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Sorting loop accesses ind[e+1] out of bounds when e = v-i-1 (the last iteration). It should be e < v-i-1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [e < v - i - 1 is correct for bubble sort; e < v - i causes access to ind[e+1] when e = v - i - 1, which is out of bounds when i=0 → e goes up to v-1, then e+1 = v → invalid]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [e goes up to v-i-1, but accesses e+1, so when e = v-i-1, e+1 = v-i which may be out of bounds if i=0 → e+1 = v, but valid indices are 0 to v-1]
